summary: Goshen Township Trustees held a special meeting and zoning workshop with the Zoning Commission and Board of Zoning Appeals to discuss potential text amendments to the Goshen Township Zoning Code. Key topics included allowing primary-use structures (barns, garages) on lots without a residential dwelling, permitting accessory structures in front yards under certain conditions, updating flag lot (panhandle lot) regulations to align with county standards, granting staff minor modification authority for small variance requests, and speeding up the BZA resolution signing process. Consultant Liz Fields facilitated the discussion and gathered direction from all boards.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda passed unanimously
Direction given to not pursue allowing accessory structures on separate adjacent lots; instead require lot consolidation
Direction to allow a primary-use storage/garage structure on R1 lots of minimum 2 acres not in a platted subdivision
Direction to set front setback at 75 feet for such structures
Direction to not require a time limit for building a residential structure on the property
Direction to adopt county standards for flag/panhandle lots (25-foot minimum width, 500-foot maximum length) instead of current 150-foot frontage requirement
Direction to implement minor modification authority for staff at approximately 10% variance with two signatures required (zoning inspector and administrator)
Direction to speed up BZA resolution signing process to eliminate the 30-day wait
action_items: Liz Fields to draft text amendment language for primary-use structures on 2+ acre R1 lots with 75-foot front setback
Liz Fields to research lot sizes and verify setback compatibility with 75-foot front setback on 2-acre lots
Liz Fields to review county shared driveway regulations for flag lot stacking situations
Liz Fields to draft flag/panhandle lot code language matching county standards (25-foot width, 500-foot length)
Liz Fields to draft minor modification authority language at 10% with two-signature requirement
Legal counsel to research whether staff can issue permits after BZA approval before resolution is formally signed
Legal counsel to advise on speeding up BZA resolution signing process and appeal process implications
Staff to follow up on camper/temporary living code amendment that was previously discussed but never formally adopted
Julia to verify county regulations on flag lots and panhandle lots
Legal counsel to clarify agricultural use definition under ORC 519.01 and its administration
key_amounts: 2-acre minimum lot size proposed for primary-use structures
75-foot proposed front setback for primary structures on qualifying lots
150-foot current minimum lot frontage in R1 district
25-foot minimum width for flag lot panhandle per county standard
500-foot maximum panhandle length per county standard
50-foot current front yard setback in R1
10% proposed minor modification threshold (reduced from initially discussed 15%)
30-day current wait period for BZA resolution signing that trustees want eliminated
notable_topics: Allowing primary-use structures (barns/garages) on vacant lots without a residential dwelling
Front yard setback requirements for structures on 2+ acre R1 lots
Flag lot / panhandle lot regulation reform
Minor modification authority for staff to handle small variance requests
Speeding up BZA resolution signing process
Barndominiums and Clermont County building code requirements (crawl space)
Agricultural use definition under Ohio Revised Code
Temporary living in campers during construction - code gap identified
Lot consolidation vs. separate lot accessory structures
Property maintenance enforcement for structures without residences
notes: Meeting was a workshop format with extensive collaborative discussion between all three boards (Trustees, Zoning Commission, BZA). A previously approved amendment allowing temporary camper living during house construction was identified as never having been formally codified - this gap needs to be addressed. Legal counsel noted that Ohio law does not allow the township to dictate the exterior appearance of structures. The discussion revealed that barndominiums are popular but face obstacles from Clermont County building codes (crawl space requirements) rather than township zoning. The transcript was truncated before the meeting concluded, so the agricultural definition discussion and any public comment portion may not be fully captured.
title: BOT Special Meeting 3-21-23.mp4
